title: paronychia capitata subsp. serpyllifolia
intro: Paronychia capitata subsp. serpyllifolia, also known as Shepherd's Needle, is a plant species in the Nyctaginaceae family, characterized by its needle-shaped leaves and small white flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as wounds, cuts, and burns. The health benefits of Shepherd's Needle include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Shepherd's Needle can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create topical applications, teas, and other remedies.
